name: diagnose-crash
description: End-to-end investigation of a crash or launch failure — the game won't start or died, not merely runs slow. Reads the log/crash-report, pinpoints the cause, dispatches the matching fix skill, then verifies. Use whenever the game crashed, won't start, closed immediately, black-screens, or the user shares an error/crash report ("崩溃","闪退","进不去","打不开","报错"); a game that runs but lags is optimize-performance, a download that never finished is fix-download-and-network.
body:
# Diagnose a Minecraft crash / launch failure

Find the real cause and **fix it yourself** when you can — don't just dump the log
back. Required skills below cover fix mechanics; this one covers the investigation.

## Step 1 — Gather evidence
1. `game(action=list)` — find the instance and its run dir.
2. `read` `<runDir>/logs/latest.log`; if huge, `grep` for `Exception`, `Error`,
   `Caused by`, `Mixin`, `OutOfMemory`.
3. `glob` `<runDir>/crash-reports/*.txt`, `read` the newest one.
4. `match_known_errors` on the key error text — trust a hit (as a strong lead; still
   cross-check against installed mods/log context before declaring a final cause,
   especially if the log/crash-report looks incomplete or truncated).
5. Can't read `latest.log` (e.g. `MalformedInputException`), it's locked, or you suspect
   the game process is hung and needs killing — **only** use `game(action=stop, instance=...)`
   (see launch-and-verify; it's scoped to HMCL-tracked processes, never the launcher itself).
   **Never** shell out to find/kill `java`/`javaw` by name or PID — HMCL-AE is itself a java
   process and can't be told apart that way; you could kill the launcher.

## Step 2 — Identify the cause

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `OutOfMemoryError`, `GC overhead` | Not enough RAM | `instance(action=set_memory, maxMemoryMB)`, ~4-6GB for modded. |
| `Incompatible magic value`, `UnsupportedClassVersion` | Wrong Java | `instance(action=list_java)` — see java-runtime. |
| `Mixin apply failed`, mod name in stack | Mod conflict | `instance(action=mods_toggle)` to disable it (never rename) — see mods-toggle-and-remove. |
| `missing dependency`, `requires fabric api` | Missing dep | `search(action=mods)` then `instance(action=mods_install)` — see mods-install. |
| `Failed to download`, asset/lib errors | Broken install | Re-install loader/version. |
| Loader/MC version mismatch | Wrong loader | `instance(action=create, gameVersion, loader)` to re-create. |
| `NoClassDefFoundError`, `NoSuchMethodError`, `IllegalAccessError` (class from an installed mod) | Mod file corrupt/incompatible/loader injection fault | Cross-check the class/package against `instance(action=mods_list)`; if it matches an installed mod, treat like a mod conflict (toggle/reinstall) — see mods-toggle-and-remove / mods-install. |
| Log timestamp is stale and no new file in `crash-reports/` | Failure happened before the game process even started (e.g. corrupt version JSON, launcher-side prep failure) | Don't keep digging in game logs; read the instance's version manifest JSON directly and check it's valid JSON. |

## Step 3 — Fix, confirm, report
- Apply via the matching required skill; confirm before anything destructive.
- Tell the user what was wrong and what changed; offer launch-and-verify to confirm.
- Can't fix it? Explain and give the smallest next step — don't dump raw logs.
- Don't ask the user to read logs; don't guess from the title, read the stack trace;
  don't reinstall everything as a first resort.
